B.name: Pesarattu
steps: Soak whole moong dal overnight. Drain well.
Blend soaked moong with ginger, green chilli, cumin and a little rice to a smooth, pourable batter. Season with salt.
Heat a flat tawa until very hot. Pour one ladle of batter and spread into a thin circle — thinner than dosa.
Drizzle oil around edges. Scatter finely chopped onion and coriander on top, press lightly.
Cook on medium heat until edges lift and bottom is golden — about 3 min. Fold and serve with ginger chutney.
B.tip: The batter should be thinner than idli batter but thicker than water. Spread quickly — moong batter sets fast.

L.name: Roasted Pumpkin Soup
steps: Preheat oven 200°C. Cube pumpkin, toss with olive oil, cumin and salt. Roast 25 min until golden and soft.
In a pot, sauté onion and garlic until soft. Add grated ginger, cook 1 min.
Add roasted pumpkin and vegetable stock. Simmer 5 min.
Blend until completely smooth. Stir in coconut cream.
Season well. Serve with crusty bread and a swirl of coconut cream.
L.tip: Roasting the pumpkin first adds depth you can't get from boiling. Don't skip it.

D.name: Spinach & Feta Frittata
steps: Preheat grill to high. Heat olive oil in an oven-safe pan (no plastic handle).
Sauté onion and capsicum 3 min. Add garlic, cook 30 sec. Add spinach until wilted.
Beat 6 eggs with salt and pepper. Pour over vegetables in the pan.
Cook on medium heat without stirring for 3 min until bottom is set.
Dot cherry tomatoes and crumble feta on top. Grill 3–4 min until top is golden and set. Rest 2 min before slicing.
D.tip: A frittata is great cold the next day — make it for dinner and take a slice to work for lunch.

B.name: Kanda Poha
steps: Rinse poha in a colander under water until just soft — about 30 seconds. Drain and set aside.
Heat oil in a pan. Add mustard seeds until they pop. Add curry leaves, peanuts — fry 1 min until peanuts are golden.
Add finely chopped onion and green chilli. Cook 3 min until translucent.
Add turmeric and drained poha. Toss gently — do not mash. Season with salt.
Cook 2 min on low heat. Squeeze lemon juice, garnish with coriander. Serve immediately.
B.tip: Do not over-soak the poha — it should be just soft, not mushy. 30 seconds under tap water is enough.

L.name: Palak Paneer
steps: Blanch spinach 1 min, ice-water shock, blend smooth.
Sauté onion, garlic, ginger golden. Add tomato, cook soft. Blend smooth.
Return blended mix to pan. Add spinach puree, simmer 5 min.
Add pan-fried paneer cubes. Cook 3 min.
Swirl in cream. Serve with roti.
L.tip: Ice water after blanching keeps spinach vibrant green.

D.name: Mediterranean Grain Bowl
steps: Cook quinoa and let cool slightly.
Roast chickpeas with olive oil, cumin and paprika at 200°C for 20 min.
Dice cucumber, halve tomatoes, chop parsley.
Assemble: quinoa base topped with all vegetables, chickpeas and crumbled feta.
Drizzle with olive oil and lemon juice. Spoon hummus on the side.
D.tip: Warm pita bread on the side makes this more filling.

B.name: Savoury Masala Oats
steps: Heat oil in a pan. Add mustard seeds and curry leaves — let them splutter.
Add cumin, then finely chopped onion. Cook 3 min until translucent.
Add green chilli, turmeric and diced tomato. Cook 2 min until soft.
Add oats and 1.5 cups water. Stir well on medium heat.
Cook 4–5 min stirring regularly until oats are thick and cooked. Garnish with coriander.
B.tip: For extra protein, stir in a beaten egg or add roasted peanuts on top.

L.name: Greek Salad with Chickpeas
steps: Drain and rinse chickpeas well.
Dice cucumber, quarter cherry tomatoes, thinly slice red onion.
Combine chickpeas, cucumber, tomatoes, olives and onion.
Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, oregano, salt and pepper for dressing.
Toss everything, crumble feta on top.
L.tip: Marinate red onion in lemon juice 10 min — removes the raw sharpness.

D.name: Shakshuka
steps: Heat olive oil in a wide oven-safe pan. Sauté onion and capsicum 5 min until soft.
Add garlic, cumin, smoked paprika and chilli flakes. Cook 1 min until fragrant.
Pour in crushed tomatoes. Season generously. Simmer 8 min until sauce thickens.
Make wells in the sauce with a spoon. Crack an egg into each well.
Cover and cook on low heat 5–8 min until whites are set but yolks still runny. Crumble feta on top. Serve with crusty bread.
D.tip: Don't rush the yolks — pull off heat when whites are just set. They finish cooking in residual heat.

B.name: Idli with Sambar & Chutney
steps: Steam idlis in greased moulds for 10–12 min until firm. A toothpick should come out clean.
For sambar: pressure cook toor dal until soft. Sauté onion, tomato and drumstick in oil. Add tamarind water and sambar powder. Add dal, simmer 10 min.
Temper mustard seeds and curry leaves in oil, pour over sambar.
For coconut chutney: blend coconut, green chilli and ginger. Temper with mustard seeds and curry leaves.
Serve idlis hot with sambar and chutney on the side.
B.tip: Use store-bought idli batter from an Indian grocery for perfect results without overnight fermentation.

L.name: Rajma Chawal
steps: Pressure cook soaked kidney beans until very soft — they should mash easily. Reserve liquid.
Sauté onion in oil until deep golden — this takes 7–8 min, don't rush it. Add ginger, garlic, cook 2 min.
Add blended tomatoes and red chilli. Cook until oil separates from masala — about 5 min.
Add beans with cooking liquid. Simmer 15 min. Mash ¼ of beans against the pot for a creamy gravy.
Serve with basmati rice, sliced onion, lemon and green chilli on the side.
L.tip: The depth of golden-brown onion is what separates good rajma from great rajma. Take your time.

D.name: Tofu & Veg Stir Fry
steps: Press tofu 10 min, cube. Pan-fry in hot oil until golden on most sides ~6 min.
Cook rice per packet. Prep all vegetables into bite-sized pieces.
In same hot pan, stir fry broccoli and carrot 3 min, then capsicum 2 min more.
Add ginger and garlic, toss 30 sec. Return tofu to pan.
Add soy sauce and sesame oil. Toss well. Serve over rice topped with sesame seeds.
D.tip: High heat, fast cooking — do not crowd the pan or vegetables steam instead.

B.name: Avocado Toast
steps: Toast 2 slices of sourdough until golden and crisp.
Halve avocado, scoop flesh into bowl. Mash with fork, add lemon juice, olive oil, salt and pepper.
Spread generously on toast. Top with chilli flakes.
Optional: add a fried or poached egg on top.
B.tip: Everything bagel seasoning on top is a game changer.

L.name: Vegetable Egg Fried Rice
steps: Use day-old cold rice — fresh rice is too wet and won't fry properly.
Heat oil in wok or large pan on very high heat. Add garlic and ginger, stir 30 sec.
Add diced carrot and peas, stir fry 2 min. Push to the side.
Crack in eggs, scramble until just set. Mix through vegetables.
Add cold rice, breaking up clumps. Stir fry 3 min. Add soy sauce and sesame oil. Toss well. Garnish with spring onion.
L.tip: High heat and cold rice are non-negotiable. Day-old rice from the fridge is best.

D.name: Dal Fry with Jeera Rice
steps: Pressure cook both dals with turmeric and salt until completely soft. Whisk smooth.
Heat ghee in a separate pan. Add cumin seeds, dried red chillies, asafoetida — sizzle 20 seconds.
Add very finely chopped onion, cook 5 min until golden. Add ginger, garlic, cook 2 min.
Add diced tomato, cook until oil separates. Pour this tarka into the dal.
For jeera rice: heat ghee, add cumin, add soaked basmati, cook 12 min on low with 1.5x water covered.
D.tip: Dal fry should be thicker and richer than dal tadka — use less water and let it reduce more.

B.name: Moong Dal Cheela
steps: Soak moong dal 2 hrs, blend with ginger and green chilli to smooth batter.
Add chopped onion, cumin, coriander and salt.
Heat non-stick tawa, drizzle oil. Pour batter, spread thin.
Cook 2–3 min until edges lift, flip, cook 1–2 min until golden.
Serve with green chutney or curd.

L.name: Dal Tadka
steps: Pressure cook toor dal with turmeric and salt until mushy. Mash and adjust.
Heat ghee — add cumin, asafoetida, red chillies — sizzle 30 sec.
Add garlic golden, then onion golden, then tomato soft.
Pour tadka over dal, stir, simmer 5 min.
Garnish with coriander. Serve with rice or roti.
L.tip: Use real ghee for the tadka — the flavour difference is dramatic.

D.name: Matar Paneer
steps: Blend onion, tomato, ginger and garlic to smooth paste.
Heat oil, add cumin. Add paste, cook until oil separates ~7 min.
Add spices. Cook 2 min. Add peas + ½ cup water. Cover 5 min.
Add paneer cubes. Simmer 4 min.
Garnish with coriander. Serve with roti.
D.tip: Golden-fry paneer cubes first for better texture and colour.

B.name: Paneer Bhurji
steps: Heat oil, add cumin, fry onion golden ~4 min.
Add ginger, garlic and green chilli. Cook 1 min.
Add tomatoes, turmeric, red chilli until mashy ~3 min.
Crumble in paneer, stir gently, cook 2 min.
Finish with garam masala and coriander.
B.tip: Add paneer last — just warm through, do not overcook.

L.name: Gongura Pappu
steps: Pressure cook toor dal with turmeric until very soft — 3 whistles.
Wash gongura leaves well. In a pot, cook gongura with onion, green chilli, garlic and a little water until completely soft — about 10 min.
Blend gongura mixture to a coarse paste. Combine with cooked dal. Adjust consistency with water.
Simmer together 5 min. Season generously — gongura is naturally sour so less tamarind needed.
Temper: heat oil, add mustard seeds, dried red chilli, curry leaves — pour over dal. Serve with rice.
L.tip: Gongura (sorrel/roselle leaves) is available at Indian grocery stores. The sourness is the soul of this dish — do not reduce it.

D.name: Dosakaya Pappu
steps: Pressure cook toor dal with turmeric until soft. Peel and cube yellow cucumber.
Add cucumber to cooked dal with tamarind extract, green chilli and salt. Simmer 10 min until cucumber is tender and slightly translucent.
Mash lightly — the texture should be between smooth and chunky.
Temper: heat oil until very hot, add mustard seeds, dried red chilli, curry leaves, garlic slices — sizzle until garlic is golden. Pour over dal.
Garnish with coriander. Serve with steamed rice and papad.
D.tip: Yellow cucumber (dosakaya) is essential — regular cucumber turns watery and lacks the slight tartness. Find it at Indian grocers.

B.name: Masala Moong Sprouts
steps: If using raw sprouts, microwave with 2 tbsp water for 90 seconds until just tender but still with bite.
Finely chop onion, tomato, cucumber and green chilli.
Mix sprouts with chopped vegetables in a bowl.
Add grated ginger, lemon juice, chaat masala and salt. Toss well.
Garnish with fresh coriander. Eat immediately or refrigerate up to 4 hours.
B.tip: Sprouts can be pre-germinated over 2 days from moong dal ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ soak overnight, drain, rinse twice daily. Or buy ready sprouts from the supermarket.

L.name: Chole with Jeera Rice
steps: Use canned or pre-cooked chickpeas. SautÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ© onion until golden. Add ginger, garlic, cook 2 min.

Add blended tomato and chole masala. Cook until oil separates ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ about 4 min.

Add chickpeas with ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ¼ cup water. Simmer 10 min until thick and saucy.
For jeera rice: heat ghee, add cumin seeds, add soaked basmati rice, cook 12 min covered on lowest heat.
Serve chole over jeera rice with coriander, lemon and sliced onion.
L.tip: Canned chickpeas work perfectly here ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ saves 45 minutes without compromising taste. Drain and rinse well.

D.name: Cheese & Veggie Quesadillas
steps: SautÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ© diced capsicum and onion in oil until soft. Add black beans and corn.
Season with cumin, smoked paprika, salt and pepper. Cook 2 min more.
Lay tortilla flat. Add cheese on one half, then vegetable mix, then more cheese.
Fold in half. Press in a dry pan on medium ~2 min each side until golden.
Serve with sliced avocado and sour cream.
D.tip: Do not overfill ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ a thin layer toasts more evenly and stays crisp.

B.name: Dahi Chivda
steps: Rinse flattened rice quickly under water. Drain immediately ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ it softens fast.
In a bowl, whisk thick curd with a pinch of sugar and salt until smooth.
Add drained poha to the curd. Mix gently.
Add finely grated ginger and chopped green chilli. Stir to combine.
Top with pomegranate seeds, coriander and a sprinkle of cumin. Eat immediately.
B.tip: Use very thick curd ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ if it is too runny, strain it through a cloth for 10 minutes first.

L.name: Spiced Chickpea Wrap
steps: Drain and rinse chickpeas. Toss with cumin, smoked paprika, garlic powder, salt and olive oil.
Pan-fry chickpeas on high heat 5 min, stirring occasionally, until slightly crispy.
Mix tahini with lemon juice and a splash of water to make a drizzleable sauce.
Warm tortilla. Layer spinach, diced cucumber, tomato, spiced chickpeas.
Drizzle tahini sauce and a spoon of yogurt. Roll tightly.
L.tip: The crispy chickpeas are the hero ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ don't skip the pan-fry step.

D.name: Baingan Bharta
steps: Roast eggplant directly on gas flame or under grill, turning, until skin is completely charred and eggplant is collapsed ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ about 15 min.
Cool slightly, peel off charred skin. Mash flesh roughly with a fork.

Heat oil, add cumin. SautÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ© onion until golden. Add ginger, garlic, green chilli, cook 2 min.
Add tomato and spices, cook until oil separates. Add mashed eggplant, stir well.
Cook 5 min on medium heat. Finish with garam masala and fresh coriander.

B.name: Muesli with Fresh Fruit
steps: Pour muesli into a bowl.
Add milk or Greek yogurt to your preferred consistency.
Top with fresh berries, sliced banana and a drizzle of honey.
Sprinkle chia seeds for extra fibre.
B.tip: Soak muesli in milk overnight ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ bircher style is creamier and more digestible.

L.name: Soya Chunks Masala
steps: Boil soya chunks 5 min in salted water, squeeze completely dry.
Fry onion golden, add ginger and garlic, cook 2 min.
Add tomatoes, red chilli and cumin. Cook until mashy.
Stir in beaten curd slowly. Add soya + 1 cup water, simmer 10 min.
Finish with garam masala and coriander.
L.tip: Squeezing soya dry removes raw smell and helps masala penetrate.

D.name: Paneer Stuffed Paratha
steps: Knead soft wheat dough with water and salt. Rest 10 min.

Mix grated paneer with green chilli, ginger, cumin, coriander, red chilli, salt ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ keep dry.

D.name: Red Lentil & Vegetable Soup
steps: SautÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ© diced onion, carrot and celery in olive oil until soft ~5 min.

Squeeze in lemon juice, season, top with parsley. Serve with crusty bread.
D.tip: Blend half the soup and stir back in for a creamy texture without cream.

B.name: Besan Chilla

Cook 3 min until edges lift and bottom is golden. Flip and cook 2 min more. Serve with curd.

B.name: Rava Upma

Add 2 cups boiling water and salt. Bring to boil.
Pour in roasted semolina gradually, stirring constantly to avoid lumps. Cook on low heat 3 min. Finish with lemon juice and coriander.
B.tip: Use boiling water ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ cold water makes lumpy upma. The roasting step is what gives it the nutty aroma.

L.name: Rasam with Steamed Rice
steps: Cook toor dal until soft. Soak tamarind in ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ½ cup warm water, extract pulp.
Boil tamarind water with chopped tomato, turmeric, salt and rasam powder for 5 min.
Add cooked dal and thin with water to a soupy consistency. Simmer 5 min.

Heat ghee ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ add mustard seeds, dried red chilli, curry leaves, garlic ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ sizzle until garlic is golden.
Pour tadka over rasam. Serve hot over steamed rice with papadums.
L.tip: Rasam should be thin and peppery ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ not thick like sambar. Add enough water to make it genuinely soupy.

D.name: Methi Dal
steps: Cook masoor dal with turmeric until soft ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ no soaking needed ~12 min.
Heat ghee, add cumin, then garlic until golden.
Add onion 4 min, then tomato and green chilli until soft.
Add chopped methi, cook 3 min until wilted.
Combine with dal, simmer 5 min. Serve with chapati.
D.tip: If methi is too bitter, sprinkle with salt, rest 10 min, squeeze out water.

B.name: Chole Bhature
steps: Pressure cook soaked chickpeas until very soft ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ 4 whistles. Reserve cooking liquid.
For chole: make a thick masala with blended onion, tomato, ginger, garlic. Cook until oil separates. Add chole masala, amchur, chickpeas. Simmer 15 min until thick.
For bhature: mix flour, curd, baking soda, salt and oil. Knead into soft dough. Rest 30 min.

Roll bhature slightly thicker than roti. Deep fry in hot oil until puffed and golden ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ about 90 seconds each.

Serve immediately ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ bhature deflate quickly.
B.tip: Bhature must be served the instant they come out of the oil. They are best eaten within 2 minutes.

L.name: Chole with Jeera Rice
steps: Use canned or pre-cooked chickpeas. SautÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ© onion until golden. Add ginger, garlic, cook 2 min.

Add blended tomato and chole masala. Cook until oil separates ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ about 4 min.

Add chickpeas with ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ¼ cup water. Simmer 10 min until thick and saucy.
For jeera rice: heat ghee, add cumin seeds, add soaked basmati rice, cook 12 min covered on lowest heat.
Serve chole over jeera rice with coriander, lemon and sliced onion.
L.tip: Canned chickpeas work perfectly here ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ saves 45 minutes without compromising taste. Drain and rinse well.

D.name: Dal Baati Churma
steps: For dal: pressure cook mixed dals with turmeric. Make a thick masala, combine with dal, simmer until thick.
For baati: mix wheat flour, semolina, ghee, salt, baking soda with enough water to make stiff dough. Shape into smooth balls.

Bake baati at 180ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ°C for 25ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ30 min turning halfway until golden and cooked through.
Crack baati open and pour warm ghee generously inside each one.
Serve baati dunked in thick dal with onion, green chilli and lemon on the side.
D.tip: Baati should have a firm, bread-like texture ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ not soft like roti.
